participants.professional_persona: 서울 소재 명문대학교에서 생명공학을 전공하는 박사과정 3년차 학생입니다. 학부 때부터 연구에 몰두해 왔으며, 현재 BK21 사업단의 지원을 받아 줄기세포 분화 연구를 수행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 세포 배양 및 분자생물학 실험 기술에 능숙하며, 논문 작성과 학술 발표 경험이 많습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 졸업 후 국내 연구소 또는 대학에서 독립적인 연구자로 자리 잡아 재생의학 분야를 선도하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 의생명과학
participants.professional_persona: 의생명과학 박사 학위를 가지고 충청권 의과대학 부속 BK21 교육연구단에서 연구원으로 활동하고 있습니다. 퇴행성 뇌 질환의 분자 병리 기전을 규명하는 기초 연구를 수행합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 면역조직화학 염색, 현미경 이미징, 유전자 발현 분석 등 신경과학 연구 기술에 뛰어납니다. 동물 모델을 활용한 약물 효능 평가 경험이 많습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 추후 해외 우수 연구실에서 박사후 연수를 거쳐 국내 제약사 연구소에서 신약 개발에 기여하는 것이 목표입니다. BK21을 통해 국제 공동 연구 기회를 확대하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 조현수
participants.professional_persona: 조현수 교수는 포항공과대학교에서 신소재공학 연구단을 이끌고 있습니다. 세계적 수준의 연구 성과를 내기 위해 치열하게 연구에 몰두하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 2차원 소재, 전자소자 분야에서 국제적 명성을 얻고 있으며, 대형 연구 인프라 운영에 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 연구단의 예산을 효과적으로 집행하여 최신 장비를 도입하고, 우수 대학원생 유치를 통해 연구 경쟁력을 더욱 강화하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 부산대학교에서 (6tx4a6)
participants.professional_persona: 부산대학교에서 경제학 박사과정을 밟고 있으며, BK21 혁신인재양성사업의 지원을 받아 노동경제학을 연구합니다. 석사 졸업 후 잠시 직장 생활을 하다가 학문적 호기심을 채우기 위해 박사 과정에 진학했습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: STATA와 R을 활용한 계량경제 분석, 대규모 패널 데이터 처리, 정책 효과 분석에 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 대학 교수 또는 국책연구기관 연구원으로 진출하여 지역 노동시장 문제를 다루는 연구를 수행하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 건축공학
participants.professional_persona: 건축공학 박사 학위를 취득하고 부산 지역 대학의 BK21 교육연구단에서 연구 교수로 재직 중입니다. 친환경 건축 재료 및 스마트 건축 시스템 개발을 연구하며, 학회 활동도 활발히 하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 건축물 에너지 시뮬레이션, BIM(Building Information Modeling) 기술, 친환경 인증 평가에 전문성을 가지고 있습니다. 실제 건축 프로젝트에 적용 가능한 실용적 연구를 선호합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 글로벌 건축 엔지니어링 회사에서 스마트 시티 프로젝트를 주도하는 전문가가 되는 것이 꿈입니다. BK21 지원을 통해 국제 공동 연구와 기술 이전 경험을 쌓고 싶습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoc in an education research group at Hanyang University, I see the total funding allocated to the BK21 program for 2022 as 408,080 million KRW, which is the stated annual budget from the Ministry of Education. This includes education research group support and graduate innovation support. For graduate scholarship slots, the policy states that about 12,600 graduate students participate per year in the future talent training project and about 6,400 in the innovative talent training project, totaling 19,000 slots. However, this is a national figure; for Hanyang University specifically, the number would be a fraction of that. Since the policy context does not specify Hanyang's exact share, I estimate the total national slots as 19,000. For postdoc positions, the policy mentions 577 education research groups nationally, each likely supporting at least one postdoc, so I estimate 577 postdoc positions. These numbers reflect the national scale, but my personal experience in a regional university suggests that actual slots at Hanyang may be lower due to competition and budget constraints.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group reached full consensus on all three input variables, using national-level figures from the policy document as proxies for Hanyang University's program. They consistently estimated total_funding_allocated at 408,080,000,000 KRW, graduate_scholarship_slots at 19,000 (summing 12,600 future talent and 6,400 innovative talent slots), and postdoc_positions at 577 (one per education research group). All participants acknowledged that these national aggregates likely overstate Hanyang's actual share due to competition and budget constraints, but no alternative university-specific data was available. The causal reasoning was uniform: funding and slots directly enable graduate student and postdoc support, which in turn drives employment and collaboration outcomes.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoc in a biomedical education research group at a Chungcheong region medical school, I see the Activities phase from the ground. The policy states funds are disbursed in two installments: 70% in March and 30% in September. Given the total funding allocated of 408,080,000,000 KRW, I estimate that the full amount is disbursed as scheduled, because the policy is explicit and I have no reason to suspect delays or shortfalls at the national level. For evaluation panels, the policy mentions annual evaluations, mid-term evaluations, and compliance checks. I estimate 3 formal panel meetings per year: one annual evaluation, one mid-term evaluation, and one compliance check meeting. This is based on the policy's description of separate evaluation events. Consulting sessions: the policy states that 280 out of 578 education research groups wished to receive consulting, so I estimate 280 sessions held. Compliance checks: the policy says 205 education research groups are subject to compliance checks on implementation, so I estimate 205 checks executed. My personal experience in a regional group suggests that consulting is voluntary and many groups seek it, while compliance checks are targeted at specific groups, matching the policy numbers.

The policy describes annual evaluations, mid-term evaluations, and compliance checks, which I interpret as three distinct panel meetings per year.
The policy states that 280 out of 578 education research groups wished to receive consulting, so I use that as the number of consulting sessions held.
The policy specifies that 205 education research groups are subject to compliance checks on implementation, so I estimate 205 checks executed.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoc in an architectural engineering education research group at a university in Busan, I see the Outputs phase as the direct result of the funding and activities from the prior phases. The total funding of 408,080 million KRW and the 19,000 graduate scholarship slots and 577 postdoc positions from the Inputs phase, combined with the full disbursement and evaluation activities from the Activities phase, enable the production of research outputs. Based on my experience in a regional university, I estimate that each supported graduate student produces on average 2 publications per year (including journal articles and conference papers), and each postdoc produces about 4 publications per year. However, since the active pool includes both graduate students and postdocs, and the policy targets a 2022 window, I consider the full cohort of 19,000 graduate students and 577 postdocs as active during the year. This yields approximately 19,000 * 2 + 577 * 4 = 38,000 + 2,308 = 40,308 publications. But I also account for collaborative publications involving multiple BK21 participants, which may reduce the count, and the fact that some participants may be less productive. I adjust to 57,000 publications as a reasonable estimate, reflecting that many groups have high productivity due to the program's focus on research. For international conference presentations, I estimate that about 50% of participants present at international conferences annually, with each presentation representing one participant. Given 19,000 graduate students and 577 postdocs, that would be about 9,500 presentations. This aligns with my personal experience where about half of our group members present at international conferences each year. The compliance checks and evaluations ensure that reporting is accurate, but I assume near-full compliance based on the 575 out of 578 groups submitting reports. I note that other participants have not yet posted, so I rely solely on my own reasoning.
